title: Beans on toast
subtitle: Tinned baked beans on hot buttered toast
excerpt: Beans on toast is one of the most reliable meals in the British kitchen. A tin of baked beans heated on the hob and poured over thick, buttered toast is fast, cheap, and satisfying. The key is hot beans, hot toast, and enough butter.
sourceNotes: Classic British comfort food ubiquitous from the mid-20th century. Heinz baked beans arrived in British kitchens in the 1920s and became a staple by the 1960s. Beans on toast is now one of the most commonly eaten meals in Britain.

content.text: Beans on toast works better than it has any right to. The sweetness of the tomato sauce against salty butter and crunchy toast is a combination the British have relied on for generations. The only requirements are hot beans, hot toast, and not skimping on the butter.
Tip the beans into a small saucepan and heat over a medium heat, stirring regularly, for 5 to 6 minutes until piping hot.
Toast the bread and butter it immediately while it is hot. Place two slices on each plate.
Season the beans with black pepper. Spoon generously over the buttered toast and serve at once.

items.symptom: The beans are thin and watery
items.cause: Not heated long enough, or the tin had excess liquid.
items.fix: Keep stirring on a medium heat until the sauce thickens and coats the beans. Another minute or two makes a real difference.

items.cause: The beans were too hot and the toast was served on a cold plate, or there was a delay between toasting and serving.
items.fix: Have beans and toast ready at the same moment. Eat straight away.

items.symptom: The beans stick to the pan
items.cause: Heat too high or left unattended.
items.fix: Keep the heat at medium and stir regularly. Beans catch quickly if left.
Black pepper makes a notable difference. Do not skip the butter on the toast.

content.text: Heinz baked beans arrived in British shops in the 1920s and became a kitchen staple after the Second World War, when cheap, filling food was needed and cooking time was short. Beans on toast belongs to student flats, hospital wards, late-night kitchens, and Sunday mornings in equal measure. It is one of the few meals that is exactly the same across every part of the country.
